mnemonics: A Method That Calls Itself Is Like A Fork That Lifts Its Own Food: Recursive, Repeating, And Requires A Base Case To Avoid An Infinite Meal.
keyPoints: Recursion is a technique where a method calls itself to solve a smaller instance of the same problem.
Every recursive method must have a base case (a condition to stop the recursion) to prevent infinite loops and stack overflow errors.
Recursion is often used for problems that can be broken down into simpler, repetitive sub-problems, like traversing tree structures or calculating factorials.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to define a base case, leading to infinite recursion and a StackOverflowError.
Not properly defining the recursive step so that the problem doesn't get smaller with each call, also leading to infinite recursion.
Using recursion for problems that are too deep, causing a stack overflow, when an iterative solution would be more efficient and safe.
